node.statement: (Special case m ≤ 2: automatic purity). For m ≤ 2 constraints on B(H): r² ≤ 3, hence r ≤ 1, so extreme points are necessarily rank-1 (pure states). More precisely, this follows from the face dimension gap: S(B(H)) has no faces of dimension 1 or 2 (the smallest positive face dimension is 3 = 2² - 1, corresponding to rank-2 states). With m ≤ 2 constraints, dim(F_{S(B(H))}(φ)) ≤ 2 < 3, which forces dim(F_{S(B(H))}(φ)) = 0, i.e., φ is pure. This is the main quantum application of Weis-Shirokov.
